prs.title: fix: remove duplicate AI thinking indicator during AI turns
prs.body: ## Description Removes the duplicate "AI is thinking..." message displayed during AI turns. ### Changes Made - Removed the redundant AI thinking status update from the game status banner. - Kept the turn badge indicator to communicate AI thinking state. - Reduced UI clutter by ensuring only one AI status indicator is shown. ## Type of Change - [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue) - [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality) - [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change) - [ ] Documentation update ## Related Issue Fixes #1851 ## Checklist - [x] My code follows the project's style guidelines - [x] I have performed a self-review of my code - [ ] I have commented my code where necessary - [x] I have updated the documentation if needed - [ ] My changes generate no new warnings - [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix/feature works - [ ] New and existing tests pass locally ## Screenshots (if applicable) ### Before Two identical "AI is thinking..." indicators were displayed simultaneously: - Game status banner above the board - Turn badge below the board ### After Only a single "AI is thinking..." indicator is displayed through the turn badge, reducing UI clutter while keeping the AI state clear to the user. ## Additional Notes This change is limited to the AI thinking state UI. The duplicate status message from the game status banner was removed while retaining the existing turn badge indicator. No gameplay logic was modified.

prs.title: fix: improve pause and resign button layout on small screens
prs.body:
## Description

Improves the responsive layout of the floating Pause and Resign buttons on very small screens (~400px width).

###Changes made:
- Repositioned the mobile FAB controls to the bottom-right corner on small screens.
- Changed the button layout from vertical to horizontal below 400px width.
- Reduced button size and spacing to minimize screen obstruction.
- Preserved accessibility and quick access to game controls.

## Type of Change

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change)
- [ ] Documentation update

## Related Issue

Fixes #1790 

## Checklist

- [x] My code follows the project's style guidelines
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my code
- [ ] I have commented my code where necessary
- [ ] I have updated the documentation if needed
- [x] My changes generate no new warnings
- [x] I have added tests that prove my fix/feature works
- [x] New and existing tests pass locally

## Screenshots (if applicable)


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/15062c5a-8639-4a7c-83da-0794fb02942e

### Before
- Pause and Resign buttons were stacked vertically on the right side and occupied noticeable screen space on ~400px width devices.

### After
- Buttons are displayed horizontally near the bottom-right corner with reduced size and spacing, resulting in a less intrusive mobile experience.

## Additional Notes

This is a CSS-only responsive improvement. No JavaScript or HTML functionality was modified.

prs.title: fix: prevent FAB overlap and add pause/play icon toggle
prs.body:
## Description

This PR fixes the overlap between the floating Pause/Resign controls and the authentication buttons on smaller screen sizes, and improves the pause button's visual feedback by toggling between pause and play icons based on the game state.

###Changes Made

- Adjusted mobile FAB positioning to prevent overlap with Sign In/Register buttons.
- Added a play icon to the floating pause button.
- Updated pause UI logic to toggle between pause (||) and play (▶) icons.
- Improved responsiveness and usability on smaller viewports.

## Type of Change

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change)
- [ ] Documentation update

## Related Issue

Fixes #1789

## Checklist

- [x] My code follows the project's style guidelines
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my code
- [ ] I have commented my code where necessary
- [ ] I have updated the documentation if needed
- [x] My changes generate no new warnings
- [x] I have added tests that prove my fix/feature works
- [x] New and existing tests pass locally

## Screenshots (if applicable)


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/29a20878-1fa0-45e0-b232-b0496b03a0c6

After
Floating controls are positioned below the authentication buttons.
Pause button correctly switches between pause and play icons when toggling game state.

## Additional Notes

This change is limited to the game board UI and does not affect gameplay logic. The implementation follows the existing FAB and pause UI patterns already present in the codebase.

prs.title: Material advantage bar
prs.body:
## Description

Added a Material Advantage Bar to provide a visual representation of the current material balance between White and Black during gameplay.

The bar updates dynamically based on the existing material point calculations and gives players a quick way to assess which side has the advantage without manually comparing captured pieces or scores.

## Type of Change

- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [x] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change)
- [ ] Documentation update

## Related Issue

Fixes #1592 

## Checklist

- [x] My code follows the project's style guidelines
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my code
- [ ] I have commented my code where necessary
- [ ] I have updated the documentation if needed
- [x] My changes generate no new warnings
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix/feature works
- [ ] New and existing tests pass locally

## Screenshots (if applicable)


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4911b5af-8a2f-4bf1-9f7d-f5e3114725c7



## Additional Notes

- The material advantage bar is based on the existing material point calculation logic.
- The indicator updates automatically whenever a capture changes the material balance.
- The visual representation helps players quickly understand the game state at a glance.

prs.title: fix: allow resign action while game is paused
prs.body:

This PR fixes the issue where users could not resign while the game was in a paused state.

Changes made:
- Allowed the resign action to remain accessible even when the game is paused
- Temporarily removed the pause overlay before showing the resign confirmation modal
- Restored the pause overlay if the user cancels the resign confirmation
- Preserved existing game-over checks to prevent invalid resign actions
- Tested the pause → resign flow for correct behavior in gameplay

prs.title: Fix OTP verification UX by displaying masked recipient email
prs.body: ## Description This PR improves the OTP verification user experience by replacing the generic email placeholder text with the registered user's masked email address. changes made : 1. Retrieved the registered user's email in the `verify_otp` view using the session user ID 2. Added masking logic to protect user privacy while still showing the recipient email 3. Passed the masked email to the OTP verification template context 4. Updated the OTP verification page to dynamically display the masked email instead of generic text 5. Verified resend OTP, invalid OTP handling, and countdown timer behavior after the change ## Type of Change - [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue) - [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality) - [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change) - [ ] Documentation update ## Related Issue Fixes #958 ## Checklist - [x] My code follows the project's style guidelines - [x] I have performed a self-review of my code - [x] I have commented my code where necessary - [ ] I have updated the documentation if needed - [x] My changes generate no new warnings - [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix/feature works - [x] New and existing tests pass locally ## Screenshot attached Added screenshots showing: 1. OTP verification page displaying masked email 2. Resend OTP working correctly 3. Invalid OTP error message display ## Additional Notes The email is masked intentionally to improve UX while maintaining user privacy.

prs.title: Fix duplicate password visibility toggle on registration page
prs.body: ## Description Fixes duplicate password visibility toggle icons on the registration page. The issue occurred because password toggle logic was implemented both in `auth.js` and inline within `register.html`, causing two eye icons to appear after entering a valid password. This PR removes the duplicate inline implementation and keeps `auth.js` as the single source of truth for password visibility toggling. ## Type of Change - [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue) - [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality) - [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change) - [ ] Documentation update ## Related Issue Fixes #957 ## Checklist - [x] My code follows the project's style guidelines - [x] I have performed a self-review of my code - [ ] I have commented my code where necessary - [ ] I have updated the documentation if needed - [x] My changes generate no new warnings - [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix/feature works - [x] New and existing tests pass locally ## Screenshots (if applicable) Before: Duplicate password visibility toggle icons appearing on registration page. After: Only a single password visibility toggle icon is displayed as expected. ## Additional Notes This was a frontend UI bug caused by duplicate password toggle rendering logic. The fix simplifies maintenance by keeping password toggle behavior centralized in `auth.js`.

prs.title: fix: resolve mobile game layout horizontal overflow on smaller screens
prs.body: ## Description This PR fixes the mobile responsiveness issue in the game interface where horizontal overflow caused sideways scrolling and layout misalignment on smaller viewport widths. ## Type of Change - [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue) - [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality) - [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change) - [ ] Documentation update ## Related Issue Fixes #708 ## Checklist - [x] My code follows the project's style guidelines - [x] I have performed a self-review of my code - [x] I have commented my code where necessary - [ ] I have updated the documentation if needed - [x] My changes generate no new warnings - [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix/feature works - [x] New and existing tests pass locally ## Screenshots Before: Layout caused horizontal overflow and sideways scrolling on smaller mobile viewport widths. After: Game interface fits properly within the viewport without horizontal scrolling, with improved responsive layout behavior. ## Additional Notes Tested in Chrome DevTools responsive mode on: - 400px - 390px - 375px - 320px This fix improves usability for smaller mobile devices by ensuring the game interface remains properly contained and responsive.

prs.title: Add theme-aware highlight colors for board themes
prs.body: ## Description This PR improves the chess board UI by making move highlight colors theme-aware. Previously, selected square and last-move highlight colors remained fixed across all board themes, causing visual inconsistency and poor UX—especially in Dark, Green, and Blue themes where the highlight colors clashed with the board palette. This update introduces theme-specific highlight colors so the board interactions remain visually consistent with the selected theme. ## Type of Change - [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue) - [ ] New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality) - [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change) - [ ] Documentation update ## Related Issue Fixes #602 ## Checklist - [x] My code follows the project's style guidelines - [x] I have performed a self-review of my code - [ ] I have commented my code where necessary - [ ] I have updated the documentation if needed - [x] My changes generate no new warnings - [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix/feature works - [x] New and existing tests pass locally ## Screenshots (if applicable) Blue Theme Dark Theme Green Theme Added screenshots demonstrating theme-aware highlight consistency across board themes. ## Additional Notes Changes made in: - `game/static/game/css/board.css` Updated: - Selected square highlight colors - Last move highlight colors - Theme-specific UI consistency for Classic, Dark, Green, and Blue themes

prs.title: fixed duplicate login card rendering on both side of the page
prs.body: ### What was the issue? The authentication page was rendering the login card multiple times due to duplicated HTML structure. ### What I fixed - Removed duplicated head and body sections - Ensured only one auth-wrapper/auth-card exists - Fixed button layout issues caused by broken CSS blocks ### Notes - No changes were made to authentication logic - No text, inputs, or button labels were modified - Google and GitHub buttons were kept unchanged Fixes #716

prs.title: fixed table overflow of json Data Types table
prs.body: **What kind of change does this PR introduce?** Bug fix & UI enhancement — makes the JSON Data Types table responsive on mobile and removes the need for horizontal scrolling. ​ **Issue Number: #1623** •Closes [🐛 Bug: Table in JSON Data Types page not adjustable for mobile #1623](https://github.com/json-schema-•org/website/issues/1623). ​ **Others?** None. **Screenshots/videos:** **Old view** (table cut off, requires sideways scrolling). **New view:** https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/862f9609-af08-4f39-9be4-e6e94999b2fe (table fits the viewport and is fully readable). ​ **If relevant, did you update the documentation?** Not applicable — this PR only updates layout and responsiveness of an existing page. ​ **Summary** This PR fixes the mobile layout of the JSON Data Types table, which previously appeared cut off and forced horizontal scrolling to read all columns. It updates the table container styles to behave responsively on narrow screens, ensures long cell content wraps correctly, and lets the table height grow naturally so it stays within the visible area on mobile devices. Desktop behavior remains unchanged. #1623 , fixes https://github.com/json-schema-org/website/issues/1623 **Does this PR introduce a breaking change?** No — this PR does not introduce any breaking changes. The updates are purely visual/responsive and do not alter existing data, APIs, or functionality. ​ **Checklist** Please ensure the following tasks are completed before submitting this pull request. Read, understood, and followed the contributing guidelines. **Tested the changes on:** •OS: macOS Sequoia 15.6.1 •Browser: Google Chrome •Version: 143.0.7499.169

issues.title: Improve Rulebook Mobile Navigation by Replacing Split-View Layout on ~400px Screens
issues.body: ## Problem Statement On screens around 400px wide, opening the Rulebook navigation menu creates a split-view layout where the navigation panel occupies one side of the screen while the lesson content remains visible behind it. This results in a cramped interface, reduces the available reading space, and can make navigation feel cluttered on smaller devices. ## Proposed Solution Adopt a mobile-friendly single-column layout for Rulebook navigation. When a user selects a topic from the navigation menu: - Close or collapse the navigation panel. - Display the selected lesson content directly below the menu area or within the main content area. - Ensure the content uses the full available width of the screen. This would eliminate the split-view experience and provide a clearer reading flow on smaller devices. ## Alternatives Considered - Keeping the current split-view layout. - Increasing the width of the content area. - Using a full-screen overlay menu that hides content while navigation is open. ## Additional Context The issue is most noticeable on screens around 400px wide where the navigation sidebar occupies a significant portion of the available space. A stacked, single-column layout would improve readability and create a more intuitive mobile experience.

issues.title: Add hover feedback for mobile menu items at ~400px screen widths
issues.body:
## Problem Statement

On screens around 400px wide, the mobile navigation menu does not provide clear visual feedback when users hover over menu items. This can make interactions feel less responsive and makes it harder to identify the currently focused option, especially when using devices or browsers that support hover interactions.

## Proposed Solution

Add a hover state to menu items in the mobile navigation layout for screen widths around 400px.

Possible hover effects include:

Background color change
Text color change
Smooth transition animation
Subtle highlight consistent with the existing design language

This would improve visual feedback and make navigation feel more intuitive.

## Alternatives Considered

Leaving the current behavior unchanged and relying solely on click/tap interactions.
Adding only an active state without a dedicated hover effect.

## Additional Context

The enhancement is intended to improve the user experience on smaller screen layouts by providing clearer interaction feedback. The hover effect should be lightweight, visually consistent with the existing UI, and should not introduce layout shifts or affect current functionality.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/31d55a9a-f17a-48e3-a531-d9a779661b69

issues.title: [Enhancement] Remove duplicate "AI is thinking..." indicators during AI turns
issues.body: ## Problem Statement During AI games, the interface displays the "AI is thinking..." status message in two different locations simultaneously: - Above the chessboard - Below the chessboard (near the material count section) Since both indicators communicate the same information, the status is duplicated and adds unnecessary visual clutter to the game interface. ## Proposed Solution Display the AI thinking status in only one location at a time. Possible approaches: - Keep only the top status banner and remove the bottom indicator. - Keep only the bottom indicator near the board information section. - Use responsive logic if different placements are needed for desktop and mobile layouts. ## Alternatives Considered - Keeping both indicators but styling one as a secondary status. - Replacing one of the indicators with a subtle loading animation instead of text. ## Additional Context ### Current Behavior When it is the AI's turn, two identical "AI is thinking..." messages are shown simultaneously on the game page. ### Expected Behavior - Only one AI thinking indicator should be visible at any given time. - The game state should remain clear without duplicating status information. - The interface should feel cleaner and less visually cluttered.

issues.title: Improve Pause and Resign button behavior on small screens (≈400px width)
issues.body:
## Problem Statement

On small screen sizes (around 400px viewport width), the floating Pause and Resign buttons remain fixed on the right side of the screen and occupy a significant portion of the available space.

This causes the controls to feel intrusive on mobile layouts and may partially obstruct gameplay or other UI elements while navigating the game page.

## Proposed Solution

- Improve the responsive behavior of the Pause and Resign floating action buttons for smaller screen widths.
- Reposition, resize, collapse, or stack the controls in a way that better fits mobile layouts.
- Ensure the buttons remain accessible without interfering with gameplay or other interface elements.

## Alternatives Considered

- Moving both controls into the existing "Game Controls" panel on smaller screens.
- Collapsing them into a single expandable floating menu.
- Reducing button size and spacing below a specific breakpoint.

## Additional Context

### Current Behavior
- At approximately 400px width, the Pause and Resign buttons remain fixed on the right edge of the screen.
- The controls take up noticeable screen space and can feel obstructive during gameplay.

### Expected Behavior
- Controls should adapt gracefully to smaller screens.
- The game board and primary gameplay elements should remain the main focus.
- Floating controls should not negatively impact usability on mobile devices.

Screen recording attached for reference.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c9e5325a-3dcb-44a3-9e7f-636de684e7e2

issues.title: Fix overlap of Pause/Resign controls with authentication buttons and add pause icon state toggle
issues.body: ## Problem Statement On the game page, the floating Pause and Resign controls overlap with the Sign In/Register buttons in the top-right corner of the interface, creating a UI layout issue. Additionally, when the Pause button is clicked, the game pauses but the button icon does not change to indicate the paused state. The pause icon (`||`) remains visible instead of switching to a play icon (`▶`), making it difficult for users to determine whether the game is currently paused. ## Proposed Solution - Reposition the Pause and Resign floating controls so they do not overlap with authentication or navigation elements. - Improve responsiveness across different screen sizes and viewport widths. - Implement icon state toggling for the Pause button: - Running state → show Pause icon (`||`) - Paused state → show Play icon (`▶`) - Ensure the icon updates correctly when the game is paused and resumed. ## Alternatives Considered - Moving the floating controls to another corner of the screen. - Hiding floating controls on smaller screens and placing them inside the Game Controls panel. - Adding a text label ("Paused") instead of changing the icon. ## Additional Context ### Current Issues 1. Pause and Resign controls overlap the Sign In/Register buttons. 2. Pause button does not visually indicate the current game state after being clicked. 3. Users may be confused about whether the game is paused or active. ### Expected Behavior - Floating controls should not overlap other UI components. - Pause button should switch to a play icon while the game is paused. - Clicking the play icon should resume the game and restore the pause icon. Screenshot attached for reference. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9ae1c361-c890-45fb-b419-63ffc1d4faec

issues.title: Add Click-to-Display Selected Block Name in Left Panel
issues.body:
## Problem Statement

Currently, when a user clicks on a block, there is no indication of which block is selected. This can make navigation and editing difficult, especially when working with multiple blocks on the canvas.

## Proposed Solution

Add a click effect that displays the name of the selected block in the left-side panel.

- When a block is clicked, it becomes the active selection.
- The selected block's name should appear in the left panel.
- The displayed name should update whenever a different block is selected.
- If no block is selected, show a default placeholder such as "No block selected".

## Alternatives Considered

- Highlighting only the selected block without displaying its name.
- Showing the block name as a tooltip on hover.

 These approaches provide some feedback but do not offer persistent visibility of the currently selected block

## Additional Context

This enhancement would improve user awareness of the active selection and make it easier to identify and manage blocks within complex workflows.

1.  Clicking a block displays its name in the left-side panel.
2.  Selecting another block updates the displayed name accordingly.
3.  The displayed name always matches the currently selected block.
4.  A default state is shown when no block is selected.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9aaf18c6-729b-48a1-808b-b79f9714d3c9

issues.title: Inconsistent hover intensity between "Why Checkora" and "Platform Capabilities" cards
issues.body:
## Problem Statement

The hover effect intensity is different between the "Why Checkora" section and the "Platform Capabilities" section. When hovering over cards, one section has a stronger/more noticeable hover effect than the other, resulting in an inconsistent user experience.

All cards across the website should follow a consistent hover intensity, including:

- Scale effect
- Shadow intensity
- Transition duration
- Background/border changes

Actual Behavior

- "Why Checkora" cards use one hover intensity.
- "Platform Capabilities" cards use a different hover intensity.
- The UI feels inconsistent when moving between sections.

Steps to Reproduce

1. Open the landing page.
2. Hover over cards in the Why Checkora section.
3. Hover over cards in the Platform Capabilities section.
4. Compare the hover animation and visual emphasis.

## Proposed Solution

Standardize the hover effects used in the "Why Checkora" and "Platform Capabilities" sections. Both sections should use consistent hover styling, including scale, shadow intensity, transition duration, and visual emphasis. This will create a more cohesive and polished user experience across the landing page.

## Alternatives Considered

- Keeping the current hover effects unchanged.
- Reducing the hover intensity of the more prominent section instead of matching both sections.
- Creating separate hover styles for each section, though this may lead to visual inconsistency.

## Additional Context

Currently, the cards in the "Why Checkora" and "Platform Capabilities" sections exhibit different hover intensities. The discrepancy is noticeable when moving the cursor between the two sections and makes the UI feel inconsistent. Screenshots or a screen recording demonstrating the difference can be attached for reference.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8ae5fe10-2886-4a6c-b4ba-e87673c03977

issues.title: Add Material Advantage Bar to Visualize Game Progress
issues.body:
## Problem Statement

Currently, players can view captured pieces and point totals, but there is no quick visual indicator showing which side holds the material advantage during a game.

Players must manually compare captured pieces or point counts to understand who is ahead. This can be difficult for newer players and reduces the ability to assess the game state at a glance.

## Proposed Solution

Introduce a Material Advantage Bar displayed alongside the chessboard.

Requirements

- Add a vertical progress bar positioned near the game board.
- The bar should visually represent the current material balance between White and Black.
- When White gains material, the White portion of the bar should increase.
- When Black gains material, the Black portion of the bar should increase.
- The bar should update automatically whenever pieces are captured.
- The indicator should be based on existing point calculations already shown in the UI.

Example Behavior

1. At game start, the bar is evenly split (50% White / 50% Black).
2. If White captures a Black pawn, the White side gains a small advantage.
3. If Black later captures a White rook, the bar shifts toward Black.
4. Large material advantages should be immediately visible without checking the score counters.

## Alternatives Considered

- Showing only numerical point differences.
- Highlighting captured-piece sections without a visual advantage indicator.


## Additional Context

Many modern chess platforms provide a material evaluation or advantage indicator to help players quickly understand the current state of the game.

This feature would improve readability, provide better visual feedback, and make the game state easier to interpret, especially for newer players.

issues.title: Add Global Light Mode Support with Theme Toggle on Home Page
issues.body:
## Problem Statement

The application currently provides only a dark-themed experience. Users who prefer lighter interfaces for readability, accessibility, or personal preference have no option to switch themes.

Additionally, there is no centralized theme management system, making it impossible for a user's visual preference to remain consistent while navigating between pages.

## Proposed Solution

Implement a global Light/Dark theme system with a theme toggle button on the Home page.

Requirements:

- Add a Light/Dark mode toggle button in the Home page navbar.
- Store the selected theme preference using local storage or user preferences.
- Automatically apply the selected theme across the entire application.
- Ensure the chosen theme persists when navigating between pages and after page refreshes.
- Apply theme styling consistently to:

- Home page
- Login page
- Register page
- Game page
- Stats page
- Shared components (buttons, cards, forms, modals, navigation bars, etc.)

## Alternatives Considered

Enable Global Light/Dark Theme Selection Across All Pages

## Additional Context

The theme selected from the Home page should act as the application's global theme preference. Once a user switches to Light Mode (or back to Dark Mode), all pages should immediately reflect that choice and maintain it throughout the session.

issues.title: Inconsistent Checkora Wordmark Colors Across Pages
issues.body: ## Problem Statement The Checkora wordmark is displayed with different color treatments across various pages of the application. On the Home page, the logo uses a balanced two-tone design, where part of the wordmark is white and part is gold. However, on pages such as Login, Register, Stats, and the Game page, the entire wordmark appears gold. This inconsistency creates a fragmented brand identity and makes the user experience feel less cohesive when navigating between pages. ## Proposed Solution - The Checkora wordmark is displayed with different color treatments across various pages of the application. - On the Home page, the logo uses a balanced two-tone design, where part of the wordmark is white and part is gold. However, on pages such as Login, Register, Stats, and the Game page, the entire wordmark appears gold. - This inconsistency creates a fragmented brand identity and makes the user experience feel less cohesive when navigating between pages. ## Alternatives Considered - Keeping page-specific logo color variations. - Using different logo styles based on page type. ## Additional Context Actual Home page logo : Currently: - Home page: mixed white + gold wordmark. - Login page: fully gold wordmark. - Register page: fully gold wordmark. - Game page: fully gold wordmark. A unified logo treatment would improve visual consistency and create a more polished user experience.

issues.title: Inconsistent logo placement between Home and Game pages
issues.body: ## Problem Statement The Checkora logo is centered in the header on the /play/ game page, while it appears correctly in the top-left on the home page. This inconsistency breaks standard web navigation conventions where the logo is expected to be a persistent top-left anchor across all pages. Users also lose the familiar "click logo → return home" affordance during an active game. ## Proposed Solution Reposition the logo on the /play/ page to the top-left of the navbar, matching the home page layout. The "VS AI" badge can remain as a small inline pill beside the logo. The Sign In / Register buttons should stay right-aligned, resulting in a standard left-logo / right-actions navbar consistent across the entire app. ## Alternatives Considered Removing the logo from the game page entirely — reduces brand visibility and removes the home navigation shortcut while in-game. ## Additional Context 1. Home page navbar: logo top-left ✅ 2. Game page header: logo centered ❌ 3. The asymmetry is visually noticeable since Sign In / Register buttons exist only on the right. ## Home page ## Game page

issues.title: Board Theme Selection Indicator Is Visually Overpowering Across Theme Options
issues.body: ## Problem Statement The current Board Theme selector uses a thick yellow highlight ring to indicate the selected theme. While it successfully shows the active selection, the indicator appears visually dominant and draws excessive attention compared to the theme previews themselves. This affects the visual balance of the Board Theme section and can make the selected-state styling feel inconsistent with the rest of the game's UI design. ## Proposed Solution Refine the selected-theme indicator to make it more visually balanced while still clearly identifying the active theme. Possible improvements include: 1. Using a thinner border or outline. 2. Using a theme-aware highlight color. 3. Adding a subtle glow or shadow effect instead of a thick ring. 4. Using a cleaner active-state design that better matches the overall UI. The goal is to maintain visibility of the selected theme without making the indicator the most prominent element in the theme selector. ## Alternatives Considered 1. Keeping the current yellow ring but reducing its thickness. 2. Replacing the ring with a subtle shadow or glow. 3. Using a neutral accent color that works consistently across all theme options. ## Additional Context The issue is purely visual and does not affect functionality. Improving the selected-state styling would enhance the overall appearance and consistency of the Board Theme selector and create a more polished user experience.

issues.title: Pause Overlay Overlaps Resign Confirmation Modal
issues.body: ## Description When a game is paused and the user clicks the Resign button, the "Resume to Continue" pause overlay remains visible while the resign confirmation modal is displayed. This causes overlapping UI elements and makes the confirmation dialog appear cluttered and difficult to focus on. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open the chess game. 2. Start a match. 3. Click Pause. 4. Click Resign. 5. Observe the UI. ## Expected Behavior 1. The resign confirmation modal should appear cleanly. 2. The pause overlay should be hidden while the confirmation modal is active. 3. Only one interactive overlay/modal should be visible at a time. ## Actual Behavior 1. The "Resume to Continue" pause overlay remains visible. 2. It overlaps with the resign confirmation modal. 3. Multiple UI layers are displayed simultaneously. ## Screenshots ## Environment - OS: macOS - Browser: Chrome - Python Version: 3.13.7 ## Additional Context The issue occurs when opening the resign confirmation dialog from a paused game state. The pause overlay is not dismissed or hidden while the confirmation modal is active, resulting in overlapping UI components.

issues.title: Add user progress dashboard with daily streak tracking
issues.body:
## Problem Statement

Currently, Checkora does not provide a dedicated user dashboard or profile progress section where players can view long-term engagement metrics.

There is no way for users to track consistency, achievements, or daily activity, which reduces motivation and long-term engagement.

A streak system would help encourage regular usage and create a stronger sense of progress.

## Proposed Solution

Introduce a user progress dashboard that includes daily streak tracking.

Suggested features:

- Add a dedicated dashboard/profile page for authenticated users
- Include a "Daily Streak" section to track consecutive active days
- Count streaks based on meaningful activity such as:
  - playing at least one game
  - completing a learning activity
- Display streak count visually using flame icons, badges, or counters
- Optionally allow users to toggle streak visibility

Potential future metrics:
- games played
- wins/losses
- AI matches played
- total play time
- achievements/badges

## Alternatives Considered

An alternative would be adding streak tracking directly on the game page.

However, long-term progress indicators are better suited for a dedicated dashboard rather than active gameplay screens.

Another alternative would be implementing only a simple streak counter without a dashboard, but a dedicated progress area would provide better scalability for future user metrics.

## Additional Context

This enhancement would improve retention, motivation, and overall user engagement by encouraging daily participation.

Example:
- User plays one game daily → streak continues
- Missing a day resets the streak

This could serve as the foundation for a broader player progress system in Checkora.

issues.title: Paused overlay persists over game over modal after reopening completed match
issues.body: ## Description After a match ends, if the user closes/leaves the game over modal and returns to the completed game screen, the pause overlay ("Resume to Continue") incorrectly remains visible on top of the game over modal. This creates overlapping UI elements and inconsistent game state behavior. The game has already ended, so pause/resume overlays should no longer remain active. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open the chess game 2. Start a match 3. Finish the game (win/loss/checkmate) 4. Leave or close the post-game modal 5. Return to the completed game screen 6. Observe the UI ## Expected Behavior - The game over modal should display cleanly - Pause overlays should be cleared once the match has ended - No overlapping modal/overlay elements should remain visible ## Actual Behavior - "Resume to Continue" pause overlay remains visible - It overlaps with the game over modal - UI state appears inconsistent even though the game has already finished ## Screenshots ## Environment - OS: macOS - Browser: Chrome - Python Version: 3.13.7 ## Additional Context This appears to be a game state cleanup issue where the paused overlay is not being removed/reset after game completion.

issues.title: Improve post-game modal UX by showing defeat-themed screen when player loses
issues.body: ## Problem Statement Currently, the same celebratory post-game modal design is shown for all game outcomes, including when the player loses to the AI. When a player loses, the interface still displays a bright victory-themed yellow modal, which creates confusing and inconsistent user feedback. A defeat outcome should feel visually distinct from a win so that the result is communicated more clearly. ## Proposed Solution Introduce a separate defeat-themed post-game modal for player loss scenarios. Suggested improvements: - Display a "YOU LOST" or similar defeat message instead of victory-style presentation - Replace the celebratory yellow glow/theme with a darker or red-themed design - Optionally use a different icon, animation, or sound effect for defeat outcomes - Keep the existing victory styling only for actual win scenarios ## Alternatives Considered An alternative would be to keep the same modal layout while only changing the result text. However, this would still make loss scenarios visually feel too similar to victory states, reducing clarity and overall user experience. ## Additional Context This is a UX enhancement aimed at improving emotional feedback and visual consistency in the game over experience. Currently: - Win → celebratory modal ✅ - Loss → same celebratory modal ❌ A dedicated defeat-style UI would make outcomes easier to distinguish and improve overall gameplay experience.

issues.title: Game over modal displays duplicate game duration after match completion
issues.body: ## Description The game over modal displays the match duration twice after a game ends. The duration is already included in the winner message, but it is also rendered again as a separate line below, causing redundant UI information. This creates clutter and reduces clarity in the post-game modal. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open the chess game 2. Start any match (PvP or vs AI) 3. Play until the match ends 4. Observe the game over modal ## Expected Behavior Game duration should be displayed only once in the modal. Example: AI (BLACK) WINS! Game duration: 2m 37s. OR Game duration shown separately, but not both. ## Actual Behavior Game duration appears twice: - once in the winner/result message - again as a separate duration line below ## Screenshots ## Environment - OS: macOS - Browser: Chrome - Python Version: 3.13.7 ## Additional Context This appears to be duplicate UI rendering in the post-game modal template or game end rendering logic.

issues.title: Game over modal shows incorrect game mode selection after vs AI match ends
issues.body: ## Description When a vs AI match ends, the game over modal incorrectly shows **PvP** as the selected game mode instead of **vs AI**. This creates confusing post-game behavior because the modal does not reflect the actual match type that was played. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open the chess game 2. Start a match in **vs AI** mode 3. Play until the game ends (AI wins / player loses) 4. Observe the game over modal ## Expected Behavior The game over modal should correctly preserve and display the completed match mode. - **vs AI** should remain selected - PvP should not be selected unless the match was actually PvP ## Actual Behavior After a vs AI match ends: - the game over modal opens - **PvP** appears selected - the actual completed mode (**vs AI**) is not reflected correctly ## Screenshots ## Environment - OS: macOS - Browser: Chrome - Python Version: 3.13.7 ## Additional This appears to be a state persistence / modal initialization issue where the selected game mode is not synchronized with the completed match state.

issues.title: Board theme/color can still be changed while game is paused
issues.body:
## Description

When the game is paused, gameplay is suspended using the "Resume to Continue" overlay, but the player can still change the chessboard theme/color.

This creates inconsistent pause behavior because the paused state should freeze active board interactions until the game is resumed.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open the chess game
2. Start a match (PvP or vs AI)
3. Click the Pause button
4. Observe the pause overlay
5. Try changing the board theme / board color
6. Notice that the board appearance still changes while paused

## Expected Behavior

1. Pausing the game should freeze board interactions
2. Board theme/color changes should be disabled while paused
3. User should only be able to resume the game before interacting with board settings

## Actual Behavior

1. Game enters paused state
2. Pause overlay appears
3. Board theme/color controls remain functional
4. Board appearance can still be changed despite paused gameplay


## Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b18b8867-cdb7-4f48-87ad-f38a2000f537

## Environment

- OS: macOS
- Browser: Chrome
- Python Version: 3.13.7

## Additional Context

Pause behavior currently blocks some gameplay interactions but does not consistently restrict board-related UI interactions.

The paused state should provide a consistent frozen gameplay experience

issues.title: Pause overlay blocks access to resign action during gameplay
issues.body:
## Description

When the game is paused, a “Resume to Continue” overlay appears over the chessboard, but the user is unable to resign while in the paused state.

The pause state effectively traps the player into resuming first before being able to end the game.

This creates inconvenient and inconsistent gameplay behavior, since resignation is a control action that should remain accessible even while gameplay is paused.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open the chess game
2. Start a match (PvP or vs AI)
3. Click the Pause button
4. Try to click Resign
5. Observe the behavior

## Expected Behavior

The Resign option should remain accessible while the game is paused so the player can directly end the match without needing to resume first.

## Actual Behavior

After pausing the game:
1. the pause overlay appears
2. gameplay is suspended
3. the player cannot resign directly
4. user must resume the game first before resigning


## Screenshots

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/94220bf6-e88e-4a94-a9c0-e79e17e9935f

## Environment

- OS: macOS
- Browser:Chrome
- Python Version: 3.13.7

## Additional Context

The pause overlay currently blocks normal game flow by forcing a resume action before allowing resignation.
Pausing should suspend gameplay interactions, but control actions like resigning should remain available.

issues.title: Unresolved merge conflict markers visible in game controls during gameplay
issues.body: ## Description During gameplay, unresolved Git merge conflict markers and unintended text are visible inside the Game Controls section. Text such as: 7d2b88cd324c3da0e5b3bb746bda11f7f35b5b81 is rendered directly in the UI along with shortcut/debug text, causing layout overflow and exposing internal development artifacts to users. This breaks the visual layout and affects the overall user experience. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open the chess game 2. Start a match (PvP or vs AI) 3. Observe the Game Controls panel on the right side 4. See error ## Expected Behavior The game controls section should only display intended controls such as: 1. Flip Board 2. Pass & Play 3. Play vs AI 4. Sound 5. Resign 6. Rulebook 7. Exit to Home No merge conflict markers, raw internal text, or overflowing content should appear. ## Actual Behavior During gameplay: 1. unresolved Git merge conflict markers are visible 2. shortcut/debug text appears unexpectedly 3. content overflows outside the intended layout 4. UI looks broken/unpolished ## Screenshots ## Environment - OS: macOS - Browser: Chrome - Python Version: 3.13.7 ## Additional Context This appears to be caused by unresolved merge conflict markers accidentally left in a frontend file (likely template / JavaScript related to game controls UI).

issues.title: Game shows victory celebration modal when player resigns
issues.body: ## Description When a player resigns from a match, the game correctly determines the winner, but the game-over modal still displays a victory-themed UI with trophy icons, celebratory styling, and victory feedback. This creates misleading user feedback because resignation represents a loss for the resigning player, not a victory. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Start a game (PvP or vs AI) 2. Play until the game controls are available 3. Click the Resign button 4. Observe the game-over modal ## Expected Behavior If the player resigns, the game should display a defeat/loss state instead of a victory celebration. For example: defeat-themed UI (e.g. red styling) defeat messaging such as "You resigned" or "Defeat" non-celebratory visual/audio feedback ## Actual Behavior After resigning, the game still shows: victory-themed popup trophy icons celebratory styling even though the resigning player lost the game. ## Screenshots ## Environment - OS: macOS - Browser: Chrome - Python Version: 3.13.7 ## Additional Context A dedicated defeat/loss state for resignation would provide clearer and more intuitive feedback to the player.

issues.title: Enhance landing page feature card layout for better visual balance
issues.body: ## Problem Statement The landing page feature cards appear visually unbalanced when the total number of cards is odd. The last card remains alone in a row, creating extra empty space and making the UI feel uneven. ## Proposed Solution Improve the feature card layout so that odd-numbered cards remain visually balanced. Possible implementation approaches: 1. Center the final card when the total number of cards is odd 2. Adjust the grid/flex layout for better alignment and spacing 3. Use a responsive CSS grid approach (auto-fit / auto-fill) to maintain symmetry across screen sizes This would create a cleaner and more polished landing page experience. ## Alternatives Considered An alternative approach would be adding a placeholder/dummy card to fill the empty space, but adjusting the layout through CSS would be a cleaner and more maintainable solution. ## Additional Context Observed on the landing page where the final feature card appears isolated, creating visual imbalance in the section layout. ## Screenshot attached

issues.title: OTP email not received after successful user registration
issues.body: ## Description After completing the registration/signup flow, the OTP verification page is shown, but the OTP email is not received in the registered email inbox. This blocks users from completing account verification and registration. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Go to the registration page 2. Enter valid username, email, and password 3. Submit the signup form 4. Redirect to the OTP verification page 5. Check the registered email inbox (and spam folder) ## Expected Behavior A verification OTP email should be sent immediately to the registered email address after successful signup. ## Actual Behavior The OTP verification page is displayed, but no OTP email is received. ## Possible Areas to Investigate - Email sending logic in the registration/signup flow - SMTP / email backend configuration - OTP generation and dispatch trigger - Silent exceptions during email sending - Session / registration flow interruptions before mail dispatch ## Environment - Browser: Chrome - OS: macOS ## Additional Context This issue blocks the user onboarding flow, as account verification cannot be completed without receiving the OTP.

issues.title: Improve OTP verification UX by displaying recipient email
issues.body: ## Problem Statement The OTP verification screen currently displays the generic message: We've sent a 6-digit code to your email address." This does not show the actual email entered during registration, which can create confusion for users—especially if they have multiple email accounts or want to confirm that the OTP was sent to the correct address. ## Proposed Solution Display the registered email address dynamically on the OTP verification page instead of the generic placeholder text. Example: Current: "We've sent a 6-digit code to your email address." Proposed: "We've sent a 6-digit code to xyz@gmail.com" This would improve user clarity and make the verification flow more transparent. ## Alternatives Considered An alternative would be partially masking the email address for privacy, such as: "We've sent a 6-digit code to un****@gmail.com" This still provides confirmation while protecting sensitive information. ## Additional Context This is a UX enhancement aimed at improving the registration/verification experience by giving users clearer feedback about where the OTP was sent.

issues.title: Registration page shows duplicate password visibility icons after entering a valid password
issues.body: ## Description On the registration page, after entering a valid password, duplicate password visibility toggle (eye) icons appear in the password section. This creates UI confusion, as only one visibility toggle should be shown for the password field. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Go to the registration page 2. Enter username and email 3. Start entering a valid password 4. Complete the password so validation checks are satisfied 5. Observe the password field area ## Expected Behavior - Only one eye (password visibility toggle) should appear for the password field. ## Actual Behavior - Two eye icons appear/collapse in the password section after entering a valid password. ## Screenshots Attached below. ## Environment - Browser: Chrome - OS: macOS

issues.title: Add hover highlight effect for “Sign In” button (yellow hover state for better visual feedback)
issues.body:
## Problem Statement

The "Sign In" button in the navigation bar currently lacks a noticeable hover interaction, making it less visually interactive and slightly inconsistent with the overall UI experience.

Users expect immediate visual feedback when hovering over clickable elements, especially primary navigation actions.

## Proposed Solution

Add a hover effect to the "Sign In" button so that:

- Background changes to yellow on hover
- Text color changes to a contrasting dark color for readability
- A smooth transition effect is applied
- Styling remains consistent with the existing design system

This enhancement will improve user interaction feedback and overall navbar usability.

## Alternatives Considered

- Adding only a border highlight on hover
- Adding a subtle shadow effect instead of background color change

However, a yellow highlight would provide clearer and more intuitive visual feedback.

## Additional Context

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/65055f26-d60d-473c-b06a-2b4685b572ac

This is a frontend UI/UX enhancement focused on improving hover state interaction for the navbar Sign In button.

issues.title: Mobile layout breaks at 400px viewport width causing horizontal overflow
issues.body: ## Description The game interface does not render correctly at smaller mobile viewport widths (tested at 400px). The layout introduces horizontal overflow, causing parts of the UI to extend beyond the visible viewport and creating an undesirable sideways scroll experience. ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open Checkora game page 2. Open browser DevTools 3. Set viewport width to 400px (mobile responsive mode) 4. Observe the layout ## Expected Behavior - Layout should fully fit within viewport width - No horizontal scrolling - Board and controls should remain properly contained and responsive ## Actual Behavior - Horizontal overflow appears - UI extends beyond viewport boundaries - Some controls/content appear clipped or poorly aligned ## Screenshots ## Environment - OS: macOS - Browser: Google Chrome - Device Simulation: Chrome DevTools Responsive Mode - Viewport Width: 400px - Python Version: 3.x (if tested locally) ## Additional Context The issue was observed while testing the game interface in Chrome DevTools mobile responsive mode at a 400px viewport width. At this screen size, the layout introduces horizontal overflow, causing sideways scrolling and making parts of the interface extend beyond the visible viewport. This negatively affects usability on smaller mobile devices. The attached screenshot in the screenshots section demonstrates the current layout behavior.

issues.title: Improve homepage navigation with clearly differentiated About and Features sections
issues.body:
## Problem Statement

The homepage currently includes "About" and "Features" links in the navigation bar, but clicking them results in only slight page movement, making the navigation feel unclear and not meaningfully differentiated.
At present:
- "About" and "Features" do not provide a clearly distinct user experience
- Users may not immediately understand what content each navigation item represents
- The feature cards reveal additional information only on hover, which reduces discoverability (especially for mobile users)

This creates confusion in the homepage navigation flow and impacts overall user experience.

## Proposed Solution

Improve homepage navigation clarity for About and Features sections

Suggested improvements:
- Create a dedicated **About** section explaining:
  - What Checkora is
  - Project purpose
  - Hybrid architecture overview
  - Technology stack / platform overview

## Alternatives Considered

- Keeping the current anchor navigation as-is
  - Rejected because the navigation feedback is minimal and may feel unclear to users

- Adding only smooth scrolling
  - Helps slightly, but does not solve the underlying issue of unclear section differentiation

## Additional Context

This is primarily a UX/navigation enhancement intended to improve homepage clarity and user flow.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cc8d4adc-b6bc-4bb3-91b2-79ce7a1a24ed

Attached a screen recording demonstrating the current navigation behavior where clicking "About" and "Features" results in minimal visual movement, making the interaction unclear.

Suggested clearer navigation flow:
Hero Section → About → Features → Rulebook/Footer

issues.title: Theme-aware move highlight colors for improved UI/UX consistency
issues.body: ## Problem Statement When switching between different chess board themes (Classic, Dark, Green, Blue), the move highlight colors (such as selected square and last-move indicators) remain fixed instead of adapting to the selected theme. This creates visual inconsistency in the UI. In some themes, especially Dark and Green, the highlight colors contrast sharply with the board palette, making the board look visually disconnected from the rest of the selected theme. For example: - A highlighted move square uses a bright fixed color that fits one theme but feels out of place in another. - The board appearance changes correctly, but move indicators do not follow the same design language. - This impacts overall UX consistency and makes the interface feel less polished. ## Proposed Solution Make move highlight colors theme-aware so they dynamically adjust based on the currently selected board theme. Possible implementation: - Define separate highlight color variables for each board theme. - Update selected square / valid move / last move highlight colors when the board theme changes. - Ensure highlight contrast remains accessible while staying visually consistent with the chosen theme. Example: - Classic theme → existing yellow-toned highlight - Dark theme → muted grey/blue highlight - Green theme → complementary lighter green highlight - Blue theme → softer blue/cyan highlight ## Alternatives Considered - Keeping a single universal highlight color across all themes. This was considered, but it leads to inconsistent UI aesthetics because one fixed highlight color does not work equally well across different board themes. ## Additional Context The issue becomes noticeable when changing board themes and making moves, where highlighted move states visually clash with the selected board colors. This is primarily a UI/UX enhancement for visual consistency and improved polish. ### Dark Theme ### Blue Theme ### Green Theme

issues.title: ✨ Enhancement: Missing Scroll-to-Bottom Arrow for Long Pages

labels.name: ✨ Enhancement
labels.description: Indicates that the issue suggests an improvement or new feature.

labels.name: Status: Triage
labels.description: This is the initial status for an issue that requires triage.

issues.body: ### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe Currently, the website includes a scroll-to-top (up arrow) button that allows users to quickly return to the top of the page. However, there is no scroll-to-bottom (down arrow) button to help users quickly navigate to the end of long pages. On content-heavy pages, this can reduce navigation efficiency and requires manual scrolling to reach the bottom. ### Steps to reproduce Visit the JSON Schema website. Click on Specifition in the navigation bar. Select JSON Schema Core from the specification options. Scroll down the page. Observe that a scroll-to-top (up arrow) button is available. Notice that there is no scroll-to-bottom (down arrow) button to quickly navigate to the end of the page. ### Describe the solution you'd like I would like to suggest adding a scroll-to-bottom (down arrow) button similar to the existing scroll-to-top button. The new button should: Appear when appropriate (e.g., when the user is near the top of the page) Smoothly scroll to the bottom of the page when clicked Follow the same design, styling, and animation pattern as the current scroll-to-top button Maintain UI consistency This enhancement would improve navigation, usability, and overall user experience, especially for long content pages. ### Describe alternatives you've considered One alternative would be to rely solely on manual scrolling or keyboard shortcuts (such as using the End key) to reach the bottom of the page. ### Additional context _No response_ ### Are you working on this? Yes

issues.title: ✨ Enhancement: Add Hover and Active Highlight Effects for “Community” and “Case Study” Navigation Items

labels.name: ✨ Enhancement
labels.description: Indicates that the issue suggests an improvement or new feature.

labels.name: Status: Triage
labels.description: This is the initial status for an issue that requires triage.

issues.body: ### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe Currently, the “Community” and “Case Study” navigation items do not display the same hover and active highlight effects as other interactive text elements and images on the page. While other navigation items and images provide clear visual feedback on hover and selection, these two items remain static. This creates inconsistency in the UI and may cause confusion about whether they are interactive or currently active. ### steps to reproduce Visit the JSON Schema website. In the navigation bar, click on “Blog.” Once on the Blog page, observe the navigation items. Hover over the “Community” and “Case Study” links. Notice that they do not display the same hover or active highlight effects as other navigation text elements. ### Describe the solution you'd like I would like the “Community” and “Case Study” navigation items to have the same hover and active state behavior as other interactive elements on the page. Specifically: Add hover effects (color change, underline, animation, or similar to existing elements) Add active/highlight styling when selected Ensure consistent visual feedback across all navigation items Follow existing design patterns used elsewhere in the UI This enhancement will improve interface consistency, usability, and user experience. ### Describe alternatives you've considered However, the main concern is visual consistency rather than functionality. A better approach would be to apply the same hover styling used for other navigation text elements, so they provide consistent visual feedback. This ensures uniform UI behavior without changing existing functionality. ### Additional context _No response_ ### Are you working on this? Yes

issues.title: ✨ Enhancement: Show down arrow (▼) when Table of Contents is collapsed

labels.name: ✨ Enhancement
labels.description: Indicates that the issue suggests an improvement or new feature.

labels.name: Status: Triage
labels.description: This is the initial status for an issue that requires triage.

issues.body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe

Currently, the Table of Contents toggle only shows an upward arrow (▲) 
when expanded. However, when the TOC is collapsed, there is no 
corresponding downward arrow (▼) to indicate that it can be expanded again.

This makes the toggle state less visually clear for users.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to the official JSON Schema website (https://json-schema.org/).
2. Click on **"Specification"** in the navigation bar.
3. Under Specification, select **"JSON Schema Core"**.
4. Scroll down to view the documentation page.
5. Locate the **Table of Contents** section on the right side.
6. Click the toggle arrow to collapse the Table of Contents.
7. Observe that there is no downward arrow (▼) shown to indicate that it can be expanded again.

### Current Behavior

- ▲ is shown when expanded
- No ▼ is shown when collapsed

### Expected Behavior

- Show ▲ when the Table of Contents is expanded
- Show ▼ when the Table of Contents is collapsed

The arrow icon should dynamically reflect the open/closed state.

### Screenshot (Current Behavior)

The image below shows the Table of Contents with only the upward arrow (▲).
When collapsed, there is no downward arrow (▼) indicating that it can be expanded again.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/15bbf1ae-9ff2-4ebd-b0fc-a75f022d75fd)


### Why This Improvement Helps

This enhancement would improve clarity, especially for first-time users, 
and make the expand/collapse interaction more intuitive.

I would be happy to submit a pull request to implement this enhancement if the maintainers agree.

### Are you working on this?

Yes

issues.title: Button text overflows outside social login buttons
issues.body: **Describe the bug** The text inside the social login buttons (e.g., “Continue with GitHub”) overflows outside the button container. This causes text clipping and misalignment, making the UI look broken and reducing readability. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Open website/pages/login.html 2. Observe the social login buttons under External Links 3. Notice that the button text does not fit inside the button boundaries and spills outside 4. See error **Expected behavior** The button text should remain fully inside the button, properly centered and readable across all screen sizes. **Screenshots** **Desktop ** - OS: macOS - Browser:chrome - Version :143.x Is this a good issue ? yes

issues.title: Login/Registered card appeared duplicate on the both side of the page
issues.body: **Describe the bug** The login/register UI is rendered twice on the same page, appearing on both the left and right sides of the screen. This creates a cluttered and confusing layout and breaks the intended single authentication card design. **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Clone the Repository 2. Open website/pages/login.html 3. Observe the page layout 4. you will see the duplicate login/registered card rendered on both sides of the page. **Expected behavior** only one login/register card should appear, ideally centered on the page **Screenshots** **Desktop** - OS: macOS - Browser: chrome - Version : 143.x is this a good issue ? yes absolutely
